root_text.sn4.22:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati silāvatiyaṁ.
root_text.sn4.22:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā samiddhi bhagavato avidūre appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharati.
root_text.sn4.22:1.3: Atha kho āyasmato samiddhissa rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn4.22:1.6: Lābhā vata me, suladdhaṁ vata me,
root_text.sn4.22:1.8: Lābhā vata me, suladdhaṁ vata me,
root_text.sn4.22:1.10: Atha kho māro pāpimā āyasmato samiddhissa cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya yenāyasmā samiddhi tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmato samiddhissa avidūre mahantaṁ bhayabheravaṁ saddamakāsi, apissudaṁ pathavī maññe undrīyati.
root_text.sn4.22:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā samiddhi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno āyasmā samiddhi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn4.22:2.2: “idhāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavato avidūre appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharāmi.
root_text.sn4.22:2.3: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn4.22:2.6: Lābhā vata me, suladdhaṁ vata me,
root_text.sn4.22:2.8: Lābhā vata me, suladdhaṁ vata me,
root_text.sn4.22:2.10: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, avidūre mahābhayabheravasaddo ahosi, apissudaṁ pathavī maññe undrīyatī”ti.
root_text.sn4.22:3.2: Māro eso pāpimā tuyhaṁ vicakkhukammāya āgato.
root_text.sn4.22:3.3: Gaccha tvaṁ, samiddhi, tattheva appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharāhī”ti.
root_text.sn4.22:3.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā samiddhi bhagavato paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.sn4.22:3.5: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā samiddhi tattheva appamatto ātāpī pahitatto vihāsi.
root_text.sn4.22:3.6: Dutiyampi kho āyasmato samiddhissa rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa …pe…
root_text.sn4.22:3.7: dutiyampi kho māro pāpimā āyasmato samiddhissa cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya …pe…
root_text.sn4.22:3.9: Atha kho āyasmā samiddhi māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn4.22:5.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ samiddhi bhikkhū”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn4.22:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans near Silāvatī.
translation_text.sn4.22:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Samiddhi was meditating not far from the Buddha, diligent, keen, and resolute.
translation_text.sn4.22:1.3: Then as Venerable Samiddhi was in private retreat this thought came to his mind,
translation_text.sn4.22:1.5: to have a teacher who is a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha!
translation_text.sn4.22:1.6: I’m so fortunate, so very fortunate,
translation_text.sn4.22:1.7: to have gone forth in a teaching and training so well explained!
translation_text.sn4.22:1.8: I’m so fortunate, so very fortunate,
translation_text.sn4.22:1.9: to have spiritual companions who are ethical and of good character.”
translation_text.sn4.22:1.10: And then Māra the Wicked, knowing Samiddhi’s train of thought, went up to him and made a terrifyingly loud noise close by him. It seemed as if the earth was shattering.
translation_text.sn4.22:2.1: Then Samiddhi went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. The Buddha said,
translation_text.sn4.22:3.2: That’s Māra the Wicked come to pull the wool over your eyes!
translation_text.sn4.22:3.3: Go back to that same place, Samiddhi, and meditate, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.sn4.22:3.4: “Yes, sir,” replied Samiddhi. He got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before leaving.
translation_text.sn4.22:3.5: And for a second time Samiddhi was meditating in that same place, diligent, ardent, and resolute.
translation_text.sn4.22:3.6: And for a second time he had the same thought …
translation_text.sn4.22:3.7: and Māra made an earth-shattering noise.
translation_text.sn4.22:3.9: Then Samiddhi addressed Māra the Wicked One in verse:
translation_text.sn4.22:4.2: from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.sn4.22:4.3: My mindfulness and wisdom are mature,
translation_text.sn4.22:4.4: my mind is serene in immersion.
translation_text.sn4.22:5.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The mendicant Samiddhi knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.

reference_text.sn4.22:1.1: bj13.218, dr15.163, ms12S1_881, msdiv158, pts-vp-pli1ed1.119, pts-vp-pli2ed1.263
reference_text.sn4.22:3.1: csp1ed12.121, csp2ed12.121, ms12S1_883, ndp12.119, pts-vp-pli1ed1.120, pts-vp-pli2ed1.264, sya15.176
